acceptance: release test execution terminates within an explicit timeout
CI output identifies the last started and last completed workspace package
timeout path captures child-process/open-handle diagnostics without publishing
candidate_pkr.objective: Bound the release test step and persist package/process evidence sufficient to identify any future stall under CI Node 24.

payload.decision: corrective PKR required for bounded CI termination and package-level hang evidence; root cause remains unresolved
evidence_refs: local replay exit 0 with final examples/invoice-triage completion
GitHub API job 86742430340 observed in_progress at step 11 at 2026-07-13T06:34:38Z
gh run log request exit 1: logs unavailable while in progress
questions_answered: The --passWithNoTests forwarding path does not hang under the available local Node 26 environment.
No local package stalled; examples/invoice-triage was last and completed.
pnpm version matches declared and CI setup; Node major differs locally from CI.
The live GitHub job remains at the root Run tests step and exposes no partial logs.
questions_unanswered: Which workspace package or post-test handle is blocking the CI Node 24 process.
Whether the behavior is Node 24-specific, runner-specific, or environment-specific.
Whether the runner completed test assertions but retained a process handle, because live logs are unavailable.

replay.observed: all 30 workspace test scripts completed; final package examples/invoice-triage passed 23 tests in 24.86s; managed Claude/Codex integration suites remained skipped

risk_implications: Release automation can occupy the concurrency slot until GitHub's default job limit despite a locally green command.
Treating local exit 0 as proof of CI health would violate AG-INTEGRITY.
A speculative product fix would risk AG-PRESERVATION and AG-PRODUCT-TRUTH without package-level CI evidence.
